title: Genotype with Quant Studio 12K Flex Protocol
author: Na Wang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.pvbdn2n

steps.instruction: Extract high-molecular-weight genomic DNA was from 2 ml venous blood using the QuickGene 610L Automatic DNA/RNA Extraction System (Fijifilm, Tokyo, Japan).

The quantitative detection of gDNA by NanoDrop 8000, the ratio of ultraviolet (UV) spectrophotometer readings at 260 nm and 280 nm wavelengths should be between 1.7 and 2.0 (i.e., A260/A280 = 1.7-2.0).

Normalize all gDNA samples so that each throughâhole receives the same input quantity of sample. Standardization of the gDNA to 96âwell sample plate with double-distilled water and the quantity of starting material is 10 ÎŒL at a concentration of 30 ng/ÎŒl.

30 independent SNPs derived from genome-wide association studies (S1 Table), were customized by Thermo Fisher Scientific company, USA. Custom TaqManÂź Assay Design Tool was used for Custom Assays design, the following is the website: https://www.thermofisher.com/order/custom-genomic-products/ tools/genotyping/ . Plate setup files (*.tpf or *.spf) contain the assay information for individual TaqManÂźOpenArrayÂź plates, including the gene symbol, gene name, assay ID, and location ofeach assay on the plate.

Track the samples Create a sample information file (*.csv) to track where the samples are in the96âwell sample plate. The OpenArrayÂź Sample Tracker Software automatically maps the sample locations from the 96âwell reaction plates to the appropriate locations in the 384âwell sample plates and TaqManÂź OpenArrayÂź plates. Then export the sample information in table format (*.csv).

Prepare the PCR mix and 384âwell sample plate a) Thaw the 96âwell reaction plate containing prepared gDNA samples at room temperature. Mix the gDNA samples by vortexing, then spin for 1 minute @ 1000 rpm. b) Mix the 2âTaqManÂź OpenArrayÂź Genotyping Master Mix by gently invertingthe bottle 10 times. c) Add the master mix 2 ÎŒL to the 384âwell sample plate. d) Using a 12âchannel pipette, transfer the normalized gDNA samples 2 ÎŒL from the96âwell reaction plate to the 384âwell sample plate. Cover the sample plate with foil, then centrifuge for 1minute @ 1000 rpm to eliminate bubbles.

substeps.instruction: Prepare the PCR mix and 384âwell sample plate
Thaw the 96âwell reaction plate containing prepared gDNA samples at room temperature. Mix the gDNA samples by vortexing, then spin for 1 minute @ 1000 rpm.
Mix the 2âTaqManÂź OpenArrayÂź Genotyping Master Mix by gently invertingthe bottle 10 times.
Add the master mix 2 ÎŒL to the 384âwell sample plate.

Using a 12âchannel pipette, transfer the normalized gDNA samples 2 ÎŒL from the96âwell reaction plate to the 384âwell sample plate. Cover the sample plate with foil, then centrifuge for 1minute @ 1000 rpm to eliminate bubbles.

steps.instruction: Prepare the QuantStudioâą 12K Flex OpenArrayÂź Plate Transfer the samples from 384-well sample plate to TaqManÂš OpenArrayÂš plates using the QuantStudioâą OpenArrayÂš AccuFillâą System. a) Confirm that the OpenArrayÂź 384âwell sample plate, OpenArrayÂź AccuFillâąSystem Loader Tips, and plate holder are completely ready and put in the right place. b) Remove an OpenArrayÂź plate from the freezer, and put in the room temperature for 10 mins. c) Prepare a syringe containing OpenArrayÂź Immersion Fluid and attach the tip to the syringe. d) Close the enclosure door, then start the QuantStudioâą OpenArrayÂź AccuFillâąSoftware. When the Remove OpenArrayÂź Plate window appears, open the instrument door, carefully remove the indicated OpenArrayÂź plate, then immediately seal the plate with OpenArrayÂź Case Lid and Load the OpenArrayÂź case with OpenArrayÂź Immersion Fluid.

substeps.instruction: Prepare the QuantStudioâą 12K Flex OpenArrayÂź Plate Transfer the samples from 384-well sample plate to TaqManÂš OpenArrayÂš plates using the QuantStudioâą OpenArrayÂš AccuFillâą System.
Confirm that the OpenArrayÂź 384âwell sample plate, OpenArrayÂź AccuFillâąSystem Loader Tips, and plate holder are completely ready and put in the right place.
Remove an OpenArrayÂź plate from the freezer, and put in the room temperature for 10 mins.
Prepare a syringe containing OpenArrayÂź Immersion Fluid and attach the tip to the syringe.

Close the enclosure door, then start the QuantStudioâą OpenArrayÂź AccuFillâąSoftware. When the Remove OpenArrayÂź Plate window appears, open the instrument door, carefully remove the indicated OpenArrayÂź plate, then immediately seal the plate with OpenArrayÂź Case Lid and Load the OpenArrayÂź case with OpenArrayÂź Immersion Fluid.

steps.instruction: Load the OpenArrayÂź plate into the instrument and run the OpenArrayÂź plates a) Place the OpenArrayÂź plate(s) on the plate adapter. Make sure that: Each plate is properly aligned in the adapter, and the plate barcode is facing up and toward the front of the instrument. b) Click 'Get Plate IDs' to import the barcode of the OpenArrayÂź plates that wewant to run. The barcode of those OpenArrayÂź plates will be populated and matched. c) Perform the Quant Studio 12K Flex platform instrument run. Temperature conditions were 10 min at 93 Â°C; cycling for 45 s at 95 Â°C, 13 s at 94 Â°C, and 2 min at 53.5 Â°C for 50 cycles, followed by incubation at 25 Â°C for 2 minutes.

substeps.instruction: Load the OpenArrayÂź plate into the instrument and run the OpenArrayÂź plates
Place the OpenArrayÂź plate(s) on the plate adapter. Make sure that: Each plate is properly aligned in the adapter, and the plate barcode is facing up and toward the front of the instrument.
Click 'Get Plate IDs' to import the barcode of the OpenArrayÂź plates that wewant to run. The barcode of those OpenArrayÂź plates will be populated and matched.

Perform the Quant Studio 12K Flex platform instrument run. Temperature conditions were 10 min at 93 Â°C; cycling for 45 s at 95 Â°C, 13 s at 94 Â°C, and 2 min at 53.5 Â°C for 50 cycles, followed by incubation at 25 Â°C for 2 minutes.

steps.instruction: After an experiment run, close the run and reâopen the *.eds file to display the Allelic Discrimination Plot screen. Then integrate the Sample names and Assay IDs into the resulting *.eds file.
Export the experiments results to anther computer. Analyze the experiment results with Taqman genotype analyzer v1.3.1.

Review the flags in the QC Summary, such as NOSIGNAL, NOAMP, OFFSCALE and et al. The flags BADROX and AMPSCORE are, by default, were not in use for the Genotyping experiment, need us manually remove these samples. View and, if necessary, change the analysis settings and Click Edit Default Settings, then specify the default call settings.
Export the analyzed data and select QuantStudioâą 12K Flex format (*.csv) for further analysis.
